title: Apply Directional Offsets on Coordinate Plane
description: Given a starting coordinate and directional instructions (north, south, east, west), compute the new coordinate by adjusting x and/or y values.
condition: A starting ordered pair (x, y) and instructions to move a certain number of units north/south/east/west.
response: Add or subtract the given number from x (east/west) or y (north/south) to produce a new ordered pair.
label: Principle / Model
example: Starting at $(11, 11)$, move 5 units south and 4 units east: $(11+4, 11-5) = (15, 6)$.

title: Compare Two Quantities on a Graph
description: Compare the y-values for two given x-values on a graph to determine which is greater or the difference between them.
condition: Given two specific x-values on a graph and their corresponding plotted points.
response: Identify the two y-values and determine which is greater or compute their difference.
label: Principle / Model
example: On day 10 the graph shows 16 pints and on day 9 it shows 2 pints, so $16 - 2 = 14$ more pints on day 10.

title: Compute Elapsed Time
description: Find the number of minutes between two times on a timeline or graph.
condition: Two clock times are given (e.g., 6:15 a.m. and 6:24 a.m.).
response: Subtract the earlier time from the later time to find elapsed minutes.
label: Principle / Model
example: From 6:15 to 6:24 is $24 - 15 = 9$ minutes.

title: Compute Northward Vertical Distance
description: Determine how many units one point is north of another by subtracting y-coordinates.
condition: Two points with known y-coordinates where one lies north (above) the other.
response: Subtract the smaller y-value from the larger to find vertical distance.
label: Principle / Model
example: If restrooms are at $y=6$ and fountain at $y=16$, then the fountain is $16-6=10$ units north.

title: Compute Total Using Constant Rate
description: Multiply a given quantity by a constant rate to find a total in a proportional relationship.
condition: A situation with a constant rate per group (e.g., 200 pounds per 12 paintings) and a multiple of the group.
response: Multiply the number of groups by the constant rate to find the total.
label: Principle / Model
example: Each group of 12 paintings weighs 200 pounds. For 5 groups (60 paintings), $5 \times 200 = 1{,}000$ pounds.

title: Describe Horizontal Shaded Region
description: Describe a shaded horizontal band using inequalities on the y-coordinate.
condition: A graph shows a shaded region between two horizontal lines.
response: State that all points have y-values greater than the lower bound and less than the upper bound.
label: Principle / Model
example: If shaded between $y=2\frac{1}{2}$ and $y=4\frac{1}{4}$, then $2\frac{1}{2} < y < 4\frac{1}{4}$.

title: Describe Vertical Shaded Region
description: Express a shaded vertical strip on the coordinate plane using x-value inequalities.
condition: Given a graph with shading between two vertical boundary lines.
response: State that all points have x-coordinates greater than the left boundary and less than the right boundary.
label: Principle
example: If the region is between $x=1.5$ and $x=6.25$, describe it as $1.5 < x < 6.25$.

title: Determine if Point Lies on Line Using Slope
description: Use slope between known points to verify whether another point lies on the same line.
condition: Given two points defining a line and a candidate point.
response: Compute slope between given points and compare with slope formed using candidate point; conclude if equal.
label: Principle
example: Slope between $(1,2)$ and $(5,6)$ is $1$. For $(9,10)$, slope from $(1,2)$ is $\frac{10-2}{9-1}=1$, so it lies on the line.
notes: Equivalent to checking consistency with linear relationship.

title: Determine Line Orientation from Constant Coordinate
description: Determine whether points lie on a vertical or horizontal line based on whether x-values or y-values are equal.
condition: Two or more points share the same x-value or the same y-value.
response: If x-values are equal, identify a vertical line; if y-values are equal, identify a horizontal line.
label: Principle / Model
example: Oak and Poplar both have height $2.4$ meters (same x-value), so their points lie on a vertical line.

title: Determine Point Closer to Y-Axis
description: Find a point that is a specified number of units closer to the y-axis by adjusting the x-coordinate.
condition: Given a point (x, y) and asked for a point k units closer to the y-axis on the same horizontal line.
response: Subtract k from the x-coordinate while keeping the y-coordinate the same.
label: Principle / Model
example: If P is (8, 6), a point 2 units closer to the y-axis is (6, 6).

title: Distance Between Axis-Aligned Points
description: Find horizontal or vertical distance between two points sharing one coordinate.
condition: Given two points with same x-coordinate (vertical) or same y-coordinate (horizontal).
response: Compute absolute difference of the differing coordinates.
label: Principle
example: Distance between $(2,1)$ and $(2,5)$ is $|5-1|=4$.

title: Find Horizontal Distance
description: Compute distance between two points sharing the same y-coordinate.
condition: Two points have equal y-values.
response: Subtract x-coordinates and take the absolute value.
label: Principle

title: Find Maximum or Minimum from Graph
description: Identify the greatest or least y-value among plotted points.
condition: Given a scatter plot of discrete data points.
response: Scan y-values and state the largest or smallest value shown.
label: Category
example: If highest point is at $2\frac{3}{4}$ cups, that is the maximum.

title: Find Missing Vertex of Rectangle
description: Determine the fourth vertex of a rectangle given three vertices with horizontal and vertical sides.
condition: Given three vertices of a rectangle aligned horizontally and vertically.
response: Use shared x- and y-coordinates to identify the fourth vertex.
label: Principle
example: If vertices are $(1,1)$, $(5,1)$, and $(5,4)$, the fourth vertex is $(1,4)$.
notes: Assumes right angles and parallel opposite sides.

title: Form Ordered Pair from Table
description: Combine corresponding x- and y-values from a table into an ordered pair (x, y).
condition: Given a row in a table listing an x-value and a y-value.
response: Write the ordered pair (x, y).
label: Production / Skill
example: If x = 4 and y = 10, write (4, 10).

title: Identify Additive Pattern Rule
description: Determine that a sequence follows a rule of adding a fixed constant to generate subsequent terms.
condition: Given a numerical sequence or coordinate table where each term increases by the same fixed amount.
response: State that the rule is to add that constant amount to each previous term.
label: Principle / Model
example: For x-values 2, 8, 14, 20, the difference is +6 each time, so the rule is "add 6."

title: Identify Equal Values in a Table
description: Scan a data table to find two entries that share the same value in a specified column.
condition: A table with numerical values in columns and a prompt asking for equal values.
response: Locate and state the two entries with matching values in that column.
label: Concept
example: Oak and Poplar both have height $2.4$ meters in the height column.
notes: Response treated as Constant because it requires naming the specific matching pair in the given table.

title: Identify Labeled Point from Ordered Pair
description: Match a given ordered pair to the labeled point located at that coordinate on a graph.
condition: A graph with labeled points and a specific ordered pair (x, y).
response: Select the label of the point plotted at (x, y).
label: Production / Skill
example: If point $F$ is plotted at $(2,0)$, then the ordered pair $(2,0)$ corresponds to $F$.

title: Identify Multiplicative Relationship
description: Determine whether one coordinate is a constant multiple of the other.
condition: Given paired values (x, y) in a table.
response: Compute ratio $\frac{x}{y}$ or $\frac{y}{x}$ and state the constant multiplicative relationship.
label: Principle / Model

title: Interpret Fractional Grid Coordinates
description: Read coordinates that include fractions or mixed numbers from a grid marked in fractional intervals.
condition: A plotted point located on grid lines marked in halves, fourths, or eighths.
response: State the ordered pair using correct fractional or mixed number notation.
label: Principle / Model
example: A point halfway between 4 and 5 on the x-axis and at $\tfrac{1}{2}$ on the y-axis is $(4\tfrac{1}{2}, \tfrac{1}{2})$.

title: Interpret Ordered Pair in Context
description: Interpret an ordered pair on a graph according to axis labels and context.
condition: A plotted point with known x-axis and y-axis meanings.
response: State the real-world meaning of the x- and y-values together.
label: Principle / Model
example: The point $(12, 200)$ means 12 paintings weigh 200 pounds.

title: Perpendicular to X-Axis is Vertical
description: Recognize that a line perpendicular to the x-axis must be vertical.
condition: Asked to draw a line perpendicular to the x-axis.
response: Draw a vertical line (constant x-value).
label: Fact
example: A line perpendicular to the x-axis has equation x = 6.

title: Perpendicular to Y-Axis is Horizontal
description: Recognize that a line perpendicular to the y-axis must be horizontal.
condition: Asked to draw a line perpendicular to the y-axis.
response: Draw a horizontal line (constant y-value).
label: Fact
example: A line perpendicular to the y-axis has equation y = 6.

title: Plot Ordered Pair
description: Plot a point by moving horizontally to the x-value and vertically to the y-value.
condition: Given an ordered pair $(x,y)$.
response: From the origin, move x units right/left and y units up/down and mark the point.
label: Rule / Plan
example: To plot $(4,5)$, move 4 right and 5 up.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Read Value from Graph
description: Read the y-value corresponding to a given x-value from a graph.
condition: A specific x-value is given and a plotted point exists at that x-value.
response: Identify the corresponding y-value from the graph.
label: Production / Skill
example: On day 11, the point is at 15 pints, so the refrigerator has 15 pints.

title: Recognize Property of Horizontal Line
description: Identify that all points on a horizontal line share the same y-coordinate.
condition: Given a horizontal line in the coordinate plane (parallel to the x-axis).
response: Conclude that all points on the line have the same y-coordinate.
label: Concept
example: On the line y = 3, every point such as (1,3) and (8,3) has y-coordinate 3.

title: Recognize Property of Vertical Line
description: Identify that all points on a vertical line share the same x-coordinate.
condition: Given a vertical line in the coordinate plane (parallel to the y-axis).
response: Conclude that all points on the line have the same x-coordinate.
label: Concept
example: On the line x = 4, every point such as (4,1) and (4,9) has x-coordinate 4.

title: Recognize Symmetric Point Pairs
description: Identify pairs of points that are mirror images across a given line of symmetry.
condition: Given a set of plotted points and a specified line of symmetry.
response: Select point pairs equidistant from the line on opposite sides with matching perpendicular alignment.
label: Production / Skill
example: If line is $x=5$, points $(3,4)$ and $(7,4)$ form a symmetric pair.
notes: Often judged visually using equal horizontal distance.

title: Reflect Across Vertical Line
description: Find the symmetric point across a vertical line of symmetry.
condition: Given point $(x,y)$ and vertical line $x=a$.
response: Keep y the same and compute reflected x so that both points are equal distance from $x=a$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If reflecting $(2\tfrac{1}{2},1\tfrac{1}{8})$ across $x=1\tfrac{1}{2}$, new x is $\tfrac{1}{2}$, so point is $(\tfrac{1}{2},1\tfrac{1}{8})$.

title: Reflect Point Over Horizontal Line
description: Find the coordinates of a point reflected across a horizontal line.
condition: Given a point $(x,y)$ and a horizontal line $y=k$.
response: Keep $x$ the same and compute reflected y so that $k$ is the midpoint between original and image y-values.
label: Principle
example: Reflect $(2,5)$ over $y=3$: new y is $1$ since $3$ is midpoint of $5$ and $1$, so image is $(2,1)$.
notes: Student may use equal vertical distance reasoning.

title: Translate Point Using Directions
description: Move a point north/south/east/west by given distances on the coordinate plane.
condition: Given starting point and directions such as 'a units east' or 'b units south.'
response: Adjust x for east/west and y for north/south by the specified amounts.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Use Area to Determine Rectangle Side Lengths
description: Use the area formula to determine possible side lengths of a rectangle.
condition: Given area of a rectangle and one vertex on a coordinate grid.
response: Choose side lengths whose product equals the area and determine remaining vertices accordingly.
label: Principle
example: If area is $36$, possible side lengths are $4$ and $9$ since $4 \times 9 = 36$.

title: Use Factor Pairs for Rectangle Area
description: Determine possible side lengths of a rectangle given its area.
condition: Given area of rectangle (e.g., 30 square units).
response: List factor pairs of the area and use them as side lengths.
label: Principle / Model
example: If area is 30, possible side lengths include $5$ and $6$ since $5\times6=30$.

title: Write Multiplicative Rule as Equation
description: Express a multiplicative relationship between x and y using an equation.
condition: Given consistent ratio between y and x values.
response: Write equation in form $y=kx$.
label: Concept
example: If $y$ is $\frac{3}{4}$ of $x$, write $y=\frac{3}{4}x$.
